title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Sharp conducts a search of Mrs. Nicolaitis’s sitting room despite her vehement objections and claims that the law does not apply to her. When Sharp attempts to search a locked cabinet, Mrs. Nicolaitis refuses to provide the key, which she has hidden under her blouse, and insults the officers. Sharp orders an officer named Cob to bring a hammer, and the cabinet is forced open, causing numerous empty brandy bottles to roll out. During Mrs. Nicolaitis's subsequent rage, Mrs. Haberdasher cleverly replaces the bottles in their original positions.

Mrs. Harbottle attempts to comfort the agitated Mrs. Nicolaitis with a sedative. Mrs. Nicolaitis expresses intense anger toward the police, comparing them to the Gestapo and complaining about the damage to her property. She dismisses the recent murder of Celia, claiming "Little Silvia" actually committed suicide due to a broken heart. While Mrs. Nicolaitis remains evasive when Mrs. Harbottle asks why she is truly uneasy, she expresses anxiety that her private cabinet has been compromised. She accuses Mrs. Harbottle of being a spy but insists she cannot leave the premises due to the ongoing murder investigation.

Meanwhile, Hercule Poirot arrives at 26 Walnut Street and is greeted by Jeronimo. Jeronimo describes the intrusive police presence and the frustration of the staff, specifically Maria. Poirot inquires about a missing light bulb from several months prior. Jeronimo recalls that the bulbs were taken around the time the police arrived to investigate an African student who had fled to the house to avoid consequences for his involvement with a woman who cheated on men for financial support.
